# sessionWatchStateDidChange(_:)

**Instance Method**

Indicates a change to the counterpart’s information.

## Declaration

```swift
optional func sessionWatchStateDidChange(_ session: WCSession)
```

## Parameters

- **session**: The session object whose state changed.

## Discussion

The session object calls this method when the value in the [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/isPaired], [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/isWatchAppInstalled], [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/isComplicationEnabled], or [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession/watchDirectoryURL] properties of the [doc://com.apple.watchconnectivity/documentation/WatchConnectivity/WCSession] object changes. Use this state to update the state of your iOS app. For example, when the complication is disabled, make a note of that fact and do not send any more data updates for the complication.

## Managing State Changes

- **sessionReachabilityDidChange(_:)**: Indicates a change to the counterpart’s reachability status.
- **sessionCompanionAppInstalledDidChange(_:)**: Indicates a change to the companion app’s installed state.
